knowledge_components.name: Quantities in proportional relationships scale together multiplicatively

complicators.name: Recognize proportional relationships in concrete contexts and scale quantities up and down by whole number and fractional factors
Determine the scale factor when given original and scaled quantities while distinguishing proportional from non-proportional situations

knowledge_components.name: Ratios represent relationships between quantities in multiple forms, with unit rates expressing these relationships per one unit to enable comparison and decision-making

complicators.name:
Identify, interpret, and write ratios from simple whole number relationships, understanding that ratios can be expressed in multiple forms. 
Flexibly work with the ratios part-to-part and part-to-whole. 
Ratios do not need to be simplified.
Write ratios involving up to one fractional quantity. Ratios are simplified.
Find unit rates from given ratios and understand their meaning in context
Use unit rates to compare quantities and determine better value or make informed decisions

knowledge_components.name:
Proportions are equations stating that two ratios are equal:
If a/b = c/d, then the ratios a:b and c:d are proportional

complicators.name: Identify and write simple proportions with whole numbers, understanding that proportions show equivalent ratios.
Write and solve proportions involving one variable.

knowledge_components.name: Percents represent parts per hundred and can be written as a fraction or decimal

complicators.name: Determine whether the proportion of a whole (represented by a percent, decimal, or fraction) is less, same, or more than the starting whole to build intuitive number sense.
Convert between percents, decimals, and fractions for whole number percents 100% and less.
Convert between percents, decimals, and fractions for whole number percents over 100%.
Convert between percents, decimals, and fractions for decimal numbers, percents 0% and greater

knowledge_components.name:
Percent equations follow the form: part = percent × whole
This relationship can be rearranged to find any unknown value

complicators.name: Find the part when given the percent and whole.
Find the percent when given the part and whole
Find the whole when given the part and percent, solving for the original total.

knowledge_components.name:
Percent change represents increase or decrease from an original value:
Percent change = (new value - original value) ÷ original value × 100%

complicators.name: Calculate and apply percent increase and decrease and know the difference between the two
Determine the outcome value after a percent change to the starting value
Work with compound percent changes and understand why they don't simply add.
